title: Ensure that the seccomp profile is set to docker/default in your pod definitions

input_row.title: Ensure that the seccomp profile is set to docker/default in your pod definitions
input_row.description: Enable docker/default seccomp profile in your pod definitions.
input_row.rationale: Seccomp (secure computing mode) is used to restrict the set of system calls applications can make, allowing cluster administrators greater control over the security of workloads running in the cluster. Kubernetes disables seccomp profiles by default for historical reasons. You should enable it to ensure that the workloads have restricted actions available within the container.
input_row.audit: Review the pod definitions in your cluster. It should create a line as below: annotations: seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod: docker/default
input_row.remediation: Seccomp is an alpha feature currently. By default, all alpha features are disabled. So, you would need to enable alpha features in the apiserver by passing "--feature- gates=AllAlpha=true" argument. Edit the /etc/kubernetes/apiserver file on the master node and set the KUBE_API_ARGS parameter to "--feature-gates=AllAlpha=true" KUBE_API_ARGS="--feature-gates=AllAlpha=true" Based on your system, restart the kube-apiserver service. For example: systemctl restart kube-apiserver.service Use annotations to enable the docker/default seccomp profile in your pod definitions. An example is as below: apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata:  141 | P a g e name: trustworthy-pod annotations: seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod: docker/default spec: containers: - name: trustworthy-container image: sotrustworthy:latest Impact: If the docker/default seccomp profile is too restrictive for you, you would have to create/manage your own seccomp profiles. Also, you need to enable all alpha features for this to work. There is no individual switch to turn on this feature. Default Value: By default, seccomp profile is set to unconfined which means that no seccomp profiles are enabled. References: 1. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/39845 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/21790 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/seccomp.md#examples 4. https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/seccomp/
input_row.impact: If the docker/default seccomp profile is too restrictive for you, you would have to create/manage your own seccomp profiles. Also, you need to enable all alpha features for this to work. There is no individual switch to turn on this feature. Default Value: By default, seccomp profile is set to unconfined which means that no seccomp profiles are enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_seccomp_profile_docker_default_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all Pods in all namespaces using the Kubernetes API (e.g., via kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json).
- For each Pod, inspect the metadata.annotations field for the presence of the annotation:
  - seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod: docker/default
- Alternatively, for newer Kubernetes versions (1.19+), check for the field spec.securityContext.seccompProfile.type: "RuntimeDefault" or "DockerDefault" (if supported).
- Pass Criteria: Every Pod definition must include the annotation seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod set to docker/default, or the equivalent seccomp profile field set to "RuntimeDefault"/"DockerDefault".
- Fail Criteria: Any Pod missing this annotation or field, or set to a value other than docker/default (or equivalent), is non-compliant.
- Example kubectl command:
  - kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.metadata.annotations."seccomp.security.alpha.kubernetes.io/pod" != "docker/default") | .metadata.name'
- The program should report all non-compliant Pods by name and namespace.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
